message:
feat(abi): port 12 glibc reserved-namespace pid/cred aliases (bd-rzk00)

Adds the 12 glibc reserved-namespace aliases for the process-id
and credentials entry points across unistd_abi and
glibc_internal_abi:

- unistd_abi: __getppid, __getuid, __geteuid, __getgid,
  __getegid, __setuid, __seteuid, __setgid, __setegid, __setsid,
  __setpgrp
- glibc_internal_abi: __gettid

glibc emits these as both internal-use entry points and as the
symbols some statically-linked NSS modules and libpthread
internals link against. Without them callers see undefined-
reference errors when linking statically.

Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers
delegating to the public counterparts — same pattern as the
previously-shipped __pthread_*, __wait* / __dup3 / __pipe2,
__opendir / __closedir, __accept / __bind / __listen, and
signal-family alias batches.

Tests: 6 abi integration tests covering: __getppid /
{__getuid, __geteuid, __getgid, __getegid} match their public
counterparts on the current process; __setuid/__seteuid/
__setgid/__setegid to current values succeed; __setpgrp /
__setsid resolve without panic (real result depends on test-
runner state); __gettid matches the existing public gettid().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Gates clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ryp2g","title":"_IO_adjust_column / _IO_adjust_wcolumn diverge from glibc — wrong column-counter algorithm","description":"## Defect

Both `_IO_adjust_column` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/io_internal_abi.rs:2055`)
and `_IO_adjust_wcolumn` (line 2091) implement their own tab-aware
column tracker:

```rust
for i in 0..count as usize {
    let byte = ...;
    match byte {
        b'\n' | b'\r' => c = 0,
        b'\t' => c = (c + 8) & !7,
        _ => c += 1,
    }
}
```

The actual glibc implementation (sunrpc-adjacent — `libio/genops.c`) is:

```c
int _IO_adjust_column (unsigned start, const char *line, int count) {
    const char *ptr = line + count;
    while (ptr > line)
        if (*--ptr == '\n')
            return line + count - ptr - 1;
    return start + count;
}
```

The semantics is: **find the position past the last newline in the
buffer, OR add `count` to `start` if no newline.** Tabs are NOT
expanded; carriage returns are NOT zeroed; the function never iterates
forward through every byte.

This is an ABI behavioral divergence. Any consumer (e.g. older glibc-
linked C++ stream code, certain pretty-printers in third-party
libraries) that calls `_IO_adjust_column` to know "how far am I from
the last newline" gets a different answer from us than from real
glibc, especially when the buffer contains tabs or carriage returns.

Additional smaller defects in the current code:
- `c + 8` and `c += 1` arithmetic on `c: u32` panics in debug mode
  for adversarial column values near `u32::MAX` (theoretical only —
  caller would need to seed `col` near INT_MAX which is implausible).
- `c as c_int` at the end can return a negative result when `c >
  i32::MAX`.

## Fix

Rewrite both helpers to mirror glibc's "scan back from end for last
newline" algorithm. Keep the membrane policy gate intact. The wide
variant scans backward over `i32` wide-chars looking for `0x0A`.

## Severity

[HIGH] (P2). Pure correctness divergence — no memory-safety
exploit, but breaks ABI compatibility for third-party libraries that
depend on the documented glibc semantics. Pretty-printer column
counters, indent trackers, and any consumer that mixes
_IO_adjust_column results with raw text-position arithmetic will
silently produce wrong layouts.","notes":"Fixed in fb6e5ed2: _IO_adjust_column and _IO_adjust_wcolumn rewritten to match glibc libio/genops.c — backward scan for last newline, returns either (count - newline_pos - 1) or (start + count). Tabs and CR no longer special-cased. Saturating arithmetic eliminates the debug-mode overflow panics in the old tab-expansion path. 6 regression tests pin glibc semantics. Verified 52/52 io_internal_native_file_test pass + clippy clean.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T01:09:59.243184856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T01:15:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T01:15:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-rz0i","title":"syscall: process_madvise invalid flags should override bad pidfd and missing iov","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL when process_madvise receives invalid flags, even when the pidfd is invalid or the iovec pointer is NULL with a nonzero vlen. Freeze that errno-precedence rule with exact ABI regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:20:38.585012373Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T19:38:33.442297284Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T19:38:33.439418305Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers delegating to the public counterparts in unistd_abi (process_abi for setpgrp on some glibcs). Tests verify each alias agrees with its public counterpart on the current process state; mutator aliases (setuid/setgid/etc) are tested only for their EPERM-on-non-root error path so they dont break the test environment.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:15:03.639884955Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:15:03.639884955Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-s170","title":"Smoke-fuzz proptests for resolver parsers","description":"Archetype 1 (Crash Detector) via proptest for 4 resolver parsers that consume arbitrary bytes: parse_hosts_line, parse_services_line, ResolverConfig::parse, encode_domain_name. Each parser must never panic, must terminate on any byte sequence, and must produce outputs bounded relative to the input size (no infinite expansion). One parser per commit. Oracle: crash-free + size-bounded output.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:16:44.821539800Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:23:12.951422556Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T20:23:12.949455593Z","close_reason":"Added 13 smoke-fuzz proptests across 4 commits covering all 4 resolver parsers: 899230c (parse_hosts_line 3 tests), db6c4a4 (parse_services_line 3 tests), 96fae93 (ResolverConfig::parse 3 tests), 547c70e (encode_domain_name 4 tests). Each target has: (a) never-panics on arbitrary bytes, (b) structural invariant check, (c) where applicable, biased structured-alphabet fuzz to reach deep parser paths. ~6500 case evaluations per run.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-ule","title":"EPIC: New runtime_math kernels (FDR, SOS, localization, Groebner, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy)","description":"Goal: add new alien-artifact kernels that compile down to tiny deterministic guards/dispatch, without blowing hot-path budgets.

Kernels in scope:
- alpha-investing / sequential FDR controller (controls false escalations under many monitors).
- SOS-derived barrier certificates (offline synthesis; runtime polynomial eval).
- Atiyah-Bott localization fixed-point policy chooser (offline fixed points; runtime selection).
- Groebner-basis normal-form canonicalization of violation signatures (offline basis; runtime reduction table).
- Blackwell approachability controller for multi-objective (latency, risk, coverage) set convergence.
- Sobol low-discrepancy probe scheduler (deterministic coverage acceleration).
- Proof-carrying policy tables (offline synth; runtime table lookup + hash/cert checks).

Non-negotiable:
- Each kernel must specify hot-path cost, update cadence, cached state surface, and a test strategy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.520777833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54.998226285Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:38.823974086Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-1v2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2vf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2wd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-3ld","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-45d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-abi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-cv9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-gn9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-l2r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1145,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: New runtime_math Kernels (How They Serve libc)

### Purpose
runtime_math is the control plane for the membrane: it decides when to stay fast vs go deep, and when to repair vs deny. New kernels are allowed only if they compile to **small deterministic runtime artifacts** (tables, tiny evaluators) with *explicit* overhead.

### Reverse Core Map Anchoring (Surface -> Failure Class -> Math -> Runtime Artifact)
These kernels must tie to concrete libc pressure points:
- **Hot string/memory kernels** -> overlap/alignment/dispatch faults -> localization + proof-carrying tables -> certified dispatch tables
- **Allocator** -> temporal/provenance corruption + contention regimes -> approachability + SOS barrier -> admissibility guard + multi-objective controller
- **Loader/symbol/IFUNC** -> compatibility drift -> Groebner normal forms + localization -> canonical “violation signatures” + resolver table witnesses
- **Conformance monitoring (many tests, many monitors)** -> multiple-testing false escalations -> alpha-investing FDR -> controller that caps false alarms across families
- **Harness probe selection** -> coverage gaps -> Sobol -> deterministic low-discrepancy probe schedules under budget

### Branch-Diversity Reminder (Project Rule)
Major milestones must include at least:
- conformal statistics: already in-tree (`conformal`, `eprocess`) + this epic adds FDR
- algebraic topology: already in-tree (`cohomology`, `persistence`)
- abstract algebra: this epic adds Groebner
- Grothendieck-Serre methods: already in-tree (`higher_topos`) + localization is in the same “geometry/localization” vein

### Runtime Cost Discipline (Non-Negotiable)
For each kernel we require:
- hot-path invocation budget (ns/op) and cadence (per call vs per epoch)
- cached state surface (what lives in `RuntimeKernelSnapshot`)
- deterministic arithmetic (fixed-point if possible; no heavy transcendentals)
- tests that show behavior invariants and that perf gates remain within budget

### Deliverable Shape
Each kernel follows the same pipeline:
1. Design bead: math contract + loss model + artifact format + invariants
2. Implement bead: tiny runtime evaluator/controller (no heavy machinery)
3. Integrate bead: wire into `RuntimeMathKernel::decide/observe` with versioned snapshot fields
4. Tests/perf bead: microbench + property tests + regression thresholds","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:51:50Z"},{"id":1146,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCompass: All 9 blockers now CLOSED. Epic complete: alpha-investing FDR, SOS barriers, localization chooser, Groebner normal form, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy tables all integrated with tests and perf validation. 809 membrane tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-unbc","title":"syscall: renameat2 invalid flags should override both-null path EFAULT with EINVAL","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for renameat2(AT_FDCWD, NULL, AT_FDCWD, NULL, 0x80000000). Add ABI regression coverage so bad flags stay dominant over null-path faults.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:19:53.600277266Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:52:21.734480678Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:52:21.732334928Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
